title: Aira conferta
taxonomy: Aira conferta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The species is further divided into several subspecies, with Aira conferta subsp. conferta being the most widely distributed. Aira conferta subsp. boreali-americana is found in North America. Aira conferta is also known to hybridize with other species in the Aira genus.

intro: Aira conferta, commonly known as silver hair grass or silver beard grass, is a perennial grass species native to the temperate regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aira conferta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aira conferta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into various supplements and formulations.
